title: 2 Thessalonians, Chapter 3 (Codex Vaticanus)
source: Codex Vaticanus via Vatican IIIF + NTVMR verse mapping + Tischendorf + KJV
description: 2 Thessalonians 3 from Codex Vaticanus (4th c.). Page-specific folios via NTVMR.

translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>Finally, brethren, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may have
free course, and be glorified, even as
it is with you: <sup>2</sup>And that we may be delivered from unreasonable and wicked men: for all
men have not faith. <sup>3</sup>But the Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep
you
 from evil. <sup>4</sup>And we have confidence in the Lord touching you, that ye both do and will do the things which we command you. <sup>5</sup>And the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God, and into the patient waiting for Christ. <sup>6</sup>Now we command you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye withdraw yourselves from every brother that walketh disorderly, and not after the tradition which he received of us. <sup>7</sup>For yourselves know how ye ought to follow us: for we behaved not ourselves disorderly among you; <sup>8</sup>Neither did we eat any man’s bread for nought; but wrought with labour and travail night and day, that we might not be chargeable to any of you: <sup>9</sup>Not because we have not power, but to make ourselves an ensample unto you to follow us. <sup>10</sup>For even when we were with you, this we commanded you, that if any would not work, neither should he eat. <sup>11</sup>For we hear that there are some which walk among you disorderly, working not at all, but are busybodies.

translation.original_text: <sup>11</sup>ἀκούομεν<S>191</S> γάρ<S>1063</S> τινας<S>5100</S> περιπατοῦντας<S>4043</S> ἐν<S>1722</S> ὑμῖν<S>5210</S> ἀτάκτως,<S>814</S> μηδὲν<S>3367</S> ἐργαζομένους<S>2038</S> ἀλλὰ<S>235</S> περιεργαζομένους·<S>4020</S> <sup>12</sup>τοῖς<S>3588</S> δὲ<S>1161</S> τοιούτοις<S>5108</S> παραγγέλλομεν<S>3853</S> καὶ<S>2532</S> παρακαλοῦμεν<S>3870</S> ἐν<S>1722</S> κυρίῳ<S>2962</S> Ἰησοῦ<S>2424</S> Χριστῷ<S>5547</S> ἵνα<S>2443</S> μετὰ<S>3326</S> ἡσυχίας<S>2271</S> ἐργαζόμενοι<S>2038</S> τὸν<S>3588</S> ἑαυτῶν<S>1438</S> ἄρτον<S>740</S> ἐσθίωσιν.<S>2068</S> <sup>13</sup>ὑμεῖς<S>5210</S> δέ,<S>1161</S> ἀδελφοί,<S>80</S> μὴ<S>3361</S> ἐγκακήσητε<S>1573</S> καλοποιοῦντες.<S>2569</S> <sup>14</sup>εἰ<S>1487</S> δέ<S>1161</S> τις<S>5100</S> οὐχ<S>3756</S> ὑπακούει<S>5219</S> τῷ<S>3588</S> λόγῳ<S>3056</S> ἡμῶν<S>2249</S> διὰ<S>1223</S> τῆς<S>3588</S> ἐπιστολῆς,<S>1992</S> τοῦτον<S>3778</S> σημειοῦσθε,<S>4593</S> μὴ<S>3361</S> συναναμίγνυσθε<S>4874</S> αὐτῷ,<S>846</S> ἵνα<S>2443</S> ἐντραπῇ·<S>1788</S> <sup>15</sup>καὶ<S>2532</S> μὴ<S>3361</S> ὡς<S>5613</S> ἐχθρὸν<S>2190</S> ἡγεῖσθε,<S>2233</S> ἀλλὰ<S>235</S> νουθετεῖτε<S>3560</S> ὡς<S>5613</S> ἀδελφόν.<S>80</S> <sup>16</sup>αὐτὸς<S>846</S> δὲ<S>1161</S> ὁ<S>3588</S> κύριος<S>2962</S> τῆς<S>3588</S> εἰρήνης<S>1515</S> δῴη<S>1325</S> ὑμῖν<S>5210</S> τὴν<S>3588</S> εἰρήνην<S>1515</S> διαπαντὸς<S>1275</S> ἐν<S>1722</S> παντὶ<S>3956</S> τρόπῳ.<S>5158</S> ὁ<S>3588</S> κύριος<S>2962</S> μετὰ<S>3326</S> πάντων<S>3956</S> ὑμῶν.<S>5210</S> <sup>17</sup>Ὁ<S>3588</S> ἀσπασμὸς<S>783</S> τῇ<S>3588</S> ἐμῇ<S>1699</S> χειρὶ<S>5495</S> Παύλου,<S>3972</S> ὅ<S>3739</S> ἐστιν<S>1510</S> σημεῖον<S>4592</S> ἐν<S>1722</S> πάσῃ<S>3956</S> ἐπιστολῇ·<S>1992</S> οὕτως<S>3779</S> γράφω.<S>1125</S> <sup>18</sup>ἡ<S>3588</S> χάρις<S>5485</S> τοῦ<S>3588</S> κυρίου<S>2962</S> ἡμῶν<S>2249</S> Ἰησοῦ<S>2424</S> Χριστοῦ<S>5547</S> μετὰ<S>3326</S> πάντων<S>3956</S> ὑμῶν.<S>5210</S>
translation.text:
<sup>11</sup>For we hear that there are some which walk among you disorderly, working not at all, but are busybodies. <sup>12</sup>Now them that are such we command and exhort by our Lord Jesus Christ, that with quietness they work, and eat their own bread. <sup>13</sup>But ye, brethren, be not weary in well doing. <sup>14</sup>And if any man obey not our word by this epistle, note that man, and have no company with him, that he may be ashamed. <sup>15</sup>Yet count
him not as an enemy, but admonish
him
 as a brother. <sup>16</sup>Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means. The Lord
be with you all. <sup>17</sup>The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write. <sup>18</sup>The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ
be with you all. Amen.
